summary:
The report now accurately reflects the current review state. Done.

## Summary

I compiled the two review critics' verdicts (requirements-conformance-critic: **pass**, 13/13 requirements; code-quality-critic: **needs_work** on one minor finding that the branch already remediates) into the PR-ready review report at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md`. Addressing the iteration-2 feedback, I verified the flagged focus-indicator issue is genuinely present and unremediated — `app/views/shared/_theme_toggle.html.erb` still uses `focus-visible:outline-*` while `ui-style-guide.md` §Accessibility baseline and every other interactive element (sign-in button, all form inputs) use the `focus-visible:ring-2 ring-indigo-600` pattern — so I added it as a new **open** finding (F2) with source, impact, and a one-line recommended fix, and updated the overall verdict (from "ship-ready" to "landable, one minor open finding recommended") and the traceability summary to match. Because the report writer doesn't own view files, F2 is flagged for a follow-up builder step rather than applied here; it's a cosmetic guide-consistency deviation, not an accessibility regression, so it doesn't block landing.
